key_insight: Standard (marginal) conformal prediction can still miss the minority class badly on imbalanced drug-discovery datasets, while class-conditional (Mondrian) conformal prediction restores per-class reliability.
clinical_relevance: For screening pipelines, this means rare but important positives may be under-protected unless coverage is checked by class, not just overall.
method: Tested marginal conformal prediction on imbalanced molecular classification tasks
Compared multiple model types: random forest, graph network, and frozen chemical language model
Applied class-conditional (Mondrian) conformal prediction as a fix
evidence_signal: Across four datasets, overall 90% coverage was met but minority-class coverage fell as low as 64.8% and 4.2%; the problem persisted across architectures and scaffold splits, and Mondrian conformal prediction restored minority coverage to target.
watch_item: Improving minority-class coverage can increase prediction-set size, so implementation should balance reliability with screening throughput and review the impact on abstention rates.
paper_title: A Quiet Failure in Calibrated Virtual Screening: Marginal Conformal Prediction Under-Covers the Minority Class, and a Class-Conditional Fix Recovers It
